texto: Sometimes I think I would rather just remember it in my head, all those streets and the places I loved. The way it smelled of car exhaust and sweet fruit. The thickness of the heat. The sound of dogs barking in alleyways. That's the Panama I want to hold on to. Because a place can do many things against you, and if it's your home or if it was your home at one time, you still love it. That's how it works.
explicacao: Citação de Cristina Henriquez sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Sometimes I think I would rather just remember it in my head, all those streets and the places I …” articula reflexao, change, freedom em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e trabalho.

texto: I took his razor from the shower floor, bits of his black hair still caked between the blades. I took his toothbrush from the sink counter and sucked on the bristles, trying to find the taste of him, but there was only the flavor of watery mint toothpaste....I pulled the sheets off the bed with the idea that I could gather up the imprint of him and save it. I thought, I can unfurl the sheets on our old bed at home. I can lie in the creases formed by his body. I can sleep with him again.
explicacao: Citação de Cristina Henriquez sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I took his razor from the shower floor, bits of his black hair still caked between the blades. I …” articula reflexao, grief, sheets em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e reflexao.

I thought, I can unfurl the sheets on our old bed at home. I can lie in the creases formed by his body. I can sleep with him again.

texto: I thought suddenly, what is the meaning of all these things? All these bags and bags I've been packing? We could take everything we have with us. We could take every single thing that every single person in the world has ever had. But not of it would mean anything to me. Because no matter how much I took and no no matter how much I had for the rest of my life, I didn't have him anymore. I could have piled everything from here straight to heaven. None of it was him.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Cristina Henriquez que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “I thought suddenly, what is the meaning of all these things? All these bags and bags I've been pa…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, materialism, could, com foco em reflexao.

texto: I wanted her to have the full, long life that every parent promises his or her child by the simple act of bringing that child into the world.

texto: Finding is for things that are lost. You don't need to find me, Mayor.

texto: Often, when people ask me what I read as a young girl, I lie. Or, I should say, I lie by omission. I tell them about my brilliant fourth-grade teacher, Miss Artis, who assigned us 'Johnny Tremain' and 'Where the Red Fern Grows' and 'Tuck Everlasting,' all books that made an impression on me. And people nod in approval.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Cristina Henriquez sobre temas ligados a educacao. A afirmação “Often, when people ask me what I read as a young girl, I lie. Or, I should say, I lie by omission…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, girl, red, útil em momentos de red e me.

texto: You shouldn't want to be like everyone else. Then you wouldn't be like you.
